content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "00_inbox/_index.json", "name": "_index.json", "extension": ".json", "mime_type": "application/json", "size_bytes": 477, "zone": "00_inbox", "sample_text": "{\n  \"title\": \"Inbox Zone\",\n  \"purpose\": \"Temporary intake area for new, ambiguous, or unclassified files before triage.\",\n  \"status\": \"active\",\n  \"zone\": \"00_inbox\",\n  \"tags\": [\"intake\", \"triage\", \"unsorted\"],\n  \"canonical_files\": [\"_README.md\"],\n  \"related_paths\": [\"00_inbox/downloads\", \"00_inbox/imports\", \"00_inbox/unsorted\"],\n  \"ai_summary\": \"Use this zone as a short-lived staging area; files should move to active, reference, archive, or system after classification.\"\n}"}]

messages.content:
```json
[
  {
    "rel_path": "00_inbox/_index.json",
    "title": "Inbox Zone Configuration",
    "content_kind": "configuration",
    "domain": "infra",
    "lifecycle": "system",
    "confidence": "high",
    "tags": ["intake", "triage", "unsorted", "zone_definition", "system_config"],
    "summary": "Defines the purpose and characteristics of the 00_inbox zone as a temporary intake area for unclassified files.",
    "entities": [
      { "name": "Inbox Zone", "type": "zone" },
      { "name": "file classification", "type": "process" }
    ],
    "relations": [],
    "recommended_zone": "40_system",
    "canonical_priority": "high"
  }
]
```

thoughts.subject: Analyzing JSON Structure
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on the structure of the JSON output. I'm working out how best to populate the array of objects, ensuring each has the requested fields like `rel_path`, `title`, and so on. The goal is to provide a complete and accurate representation of the classified data within the specified JSON format.

thoughts.subject: Mapping Field Attributes
thoughts.description: I've started mapping the data to the output fields. I am currently focusing on extracting attributes, using the `sample_text` and `rel_path` from the input to infer `title`, `content_kind`, `domain`, `lifecycle`, and `confidence`. My current process prioritizes using the provided text to inform the output, followed by careful selection of suitable options from guidance. I'm aiming for accuracy in representing the data.

thoughts.subject: Classifying Data Points
thoughts.description: My focus is now on the classification process for each field. I've broken down how I'll extract and infer data from the input `rel_path` and `sample_text`. I'm determining the appropriate values for each field, like `title`, `content_kind`, and the other required attributes. My approach is to deduce values from the text and guidance to make a JSON array of objects. I'm prioritizing accuracy in this process.
